units.subject: Hindi
topics: Understanding general ability to comprehend words and phrases in appropriate contexts
Comprehending short coherent statements in appropriate contexts
Understanding explicit or implicit messages in both contexts
Understanding long statements with sufficient accuracy and mentally organizing them
Understanding and analyzing the meaning of complex statements
subtopics: Answering questions asked by the examiner orally based on understanding of a given examination/oral test
Using appropriate language for three types of contexts suitable for the subject
Selecting topics related to personal experience such as a joke, funny story, recently read book, or movie

units.subject: Hindi
topics: Skimming to grasp the central idea of the passage
Silent reading with concentration on a specific task
Expressing opinion on the read material
Appreciating language, theme, and style
Developing interest in community
Developing reading skills according to different types of community
Identifying meaning differences of words according to context
subtopics: Understanding relationships between different parts of the read material
Giving headings and subheadings to read paragraphs
Transforming prose based on elements like rhyme, rhythm, metaphor, imagery, hyperbole

units.subject: Hindi
topics: Behaving according to the standard form of the letter
Using appropriate punctuation marks
Using emotional language and writing style naturally
Dividing into appropriate paragraphs for writing
Writing various types of letters (letter of application, congratulatory, condolence, email, order, SMS) and filling them
Gathering necessary material from various sources to write on a specific topic
Describing observed events and giving opinions
Translating from one language mode to another
Preparing speeches for functions and meetings
Writing summaries, abridgments, and emotional content
Explaining prose and poetry excerpts
Expressing personal thoughts and emotions clearly and simply
Maintaining coherence and unity of thought
Bringing creativity in writing
Producing readable text without unnecessary cutting
Comparing two different passages and expressing views on their interconnection
subtopics: Answering high-order thinking questions based on knowledge, position, or higher ability easily
Writing paragraphs with completeness (connecting all aspects of the topic within limited size)
Showing coherence through thoughts and logical sequence
Keeping the paragraph connected from beginning to end by a single thread
Expressing the complete matter related to the topic in limited words without unnecessary expansion

units.subject: Hindi
topics: Informal Letter: Tone and style (friendly, simple, concise, modest)
Formal Letter: Developing ability to convey messages for work, business, dialogue, advice, request, suggestion in different situations
Simple and conversational language style with appropriate, accurate words
Clear, connected thoughts with necessary formalities; presenting facts concisely and completely
Advertisement Writing: Mentioning various qualities of the product
Attractive writing style
Novelty in the advertisement, connection to current times, and difference from others
Use of slogans as needed
Use of boxes, lines, or colors (optional but can make presentation attractive)
Dialogue Writing: Connected and purposeful dialogue within limits
Language style suitable for characters
Indication of gestures in the character
Completing the topic/issue by the end of the dialogue
Short Story Writing: Story quality, variety, curiosity/imagination
Character-appropriate dialogue
Purposefulness
Letter of Congratulations: Connection to topic, concise and meaningful
Language proficiency and presentation
Creativity/Imagination
Use of poetry lines suitable for the topic (optional)
Email Writing: Understandable language, connection to topic, concise and meaningful
Adherence to formal structure
Self-Introduction Writing: Clear, complete, and appropriate content
Mentioning name, date of birth, current address, educational qualification, experience, interests, autobiography, phone number (confidentiality expected in exam)
Other special information/qualifications
Information Writing: Simple and understandable language, clarity of topic, complete information related to topic, adherence to formal structure
